reason: The item targets a clear boundary-aware judgment: rejecting a single-parameter safety claim when a second parameter lies in an uncharacterized interval, and it properly asks for the missing information needed to resolve that uncertainty. However, as a SHORT_ANSWER it provides no explicit decoy set, limiting discriminative signal calibration and making scoring more rubric-dependent than option-dependent. Minor wording ambiguity (aged conductivity stated as “increased to 0.044” which equals the original) risks distracting from the intended multi-parameter uncertainty focus.
